title: Mystroxylon englerianum
intro: Mystroxylon englerianum, also known as the 'Kampo' plant, is a shrub or small tree native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which are believed to help treat a range of conditions including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Mystroxylon englerianum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Mystroxylon englerianum is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall health and well-being.
